Class BaseUnit

java.lang.Object
logic.unit.BaseUnit
All Implemented Interfaces:
Flammable
Direct Known Subclasses:
LeaderUnit
public class BaseUnit extends Object implements Flammable
Represents a base entity on the game board. This can be a combat unit (Attacker/Defender), an environmental obstacle, or a special structure like a Town. Handles the entity's stats, state, inventory of skills/effects, and interaction with the grid.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    BaseUnit(UnitStats stats, Team team)
    Constructs a new BaseUnit using the provided stat blueprint and assigns it to a team.

    Sets the unit on fire by adding a BurnEffect to its queue, provided it is flammable and not currently protected by airborne status (FlyingMovement).

    Automatically constructs and sets the sprite path by determining the unit's role (obstacle or rookie) and appending the provided filename.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • BaseUnit

      public BaseUnit(UnitStats stats, Team team)
      Constructs a new BaseUnit using the provided stat blueprint and assigns it to a team. Safely creates deep copies of the unit's passive skills to prevent cross-game contamination.
      Parameters:
      stats - The UnitStats blueprint containing the default values for this unit type.
      team - The Team this unit belongs to.

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Retrieves the unique identifier of this unit type.
      Returns:
      The string ID of the unit.

    • setId

      public void setId(String id)
      Sets the unique identifier for this unit type.
      Parameters:
      id - The string ID to assign.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Retrieves the display name of this unit.
      Returns:
      The unit's display name.

    • setName

      public void setName(String name)
      Sets the display name for this unit.
      Parameters:
      name - The new display name.

    • getSpritePath

      public String getSpritePath()
      Retrieves the file path for the sprite image representing this unit.
      Returns:
      The relative string path to the sprite asset.

    • setSpritePath

      public void setSpritePath(String spritePath)
      Directly assigns the exact file path for the sprite image.
      Parameters:
      spritePath - The full relative path to the image.

    • setSpriteFile

      public void setSpriteFile(String spriteFile)
      Automatically constructs and sets the sprite path by determining the unit's role (obstacle or rookie) and appending the provided filename.
      Parameters:
      spriteFile - The name of the specific sprite file (e.g., "knight.png").

    • getPosition

      public GridPoint getPosition()
      Retrieves the current grid coordinates of the unit.
      Returns:
      The GridPoint representing the unit's location.

    • getRow

      public int getRow()
      Retrieves the specific row index where the unit is located.
      Returns:
      The integer row coordinate.

    • getCol

      public int getCol()
      Retrieves the specific column index where the unit is located.
      Returns:
      The integer column coordinate.

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(int rol, int col)
      Updates the unit's position on the grid using primitive coordinates.
      Parameters:
      rol - The row index.
      col - The column index.

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(GridPoint position)
      Updates the unit's position on the grid using a GridPoint object.
      Parameters:
      position - The new GridPoint to assign to the unit.

    • getTeam

      public Team getTeam()
      Retrieves the team affiliation of this unit.
      Returns:
      The Team enum representing the unit's allegiance.

    • setTeam

      public void setTeam(Team team)
      Sets the team affiliation of this unit.
      Parameters:
      team - The Team enum to assign.

    • getMaxHp

      public int getMaxHp()
      Retrieves the maximum health points this unit can have.
      Returns:
      The maximum HP value.

    • setMaxHp

      public void setMaxHp(int maxHp)
      Sets the unit's maximum health pool. If the new maximum is lower than the unit's current HP, the current HP is scaled down to match the new maximum. Minimum allowed value is 0.
      Parameters:
      maxHp - The new maximum health value.

    • getHp

      public int getHp()
      Retrieves the current health points of the unit.
      Returns:
      The current HP value.

    • setHp

      public void setHp(int hp)
      Sets the unit's current health points, firmly clamping the value between 0 and the unit's maximum HP.
      Parameters:
      hp - The target health value.

    • getAttackPower

      public int getAttackPower()
      Retrieves the raw offensive power stat of the unit.
      Returns:
      The integer attack power.

    • setAttackPower

      public void setAttackPower(int atk)
      Sets the unit's attack power. Ensures the stat cannot drop below 0.
      Parameters:
      atk - The new attack power value.

    • getDefensePower

      public int getDefensePower()
      Retrieves the defensive mitigation stat of the unit.
      Returns:
      The integer defense power.

    • setDefensePower

      public void setDefensePower(int def)
      Sets the unit's defense power. Ensures the stat cannot drop below 0.
      Parameters:
      def - The new defense power value.

    • getMoveRange

      public int getMoveRange()
      Retrieves the maximum number of tiles this unit can traverse in a single turn.
      Returns:
      The movement range.

    • setMoveRange

      public void setMoveRange(int moveRange)
      Sets the unit's movement range. Ensures the stat cannot drop below 0.
      Parameters:
      moveRange - The new movement range value.

    • getAttackRange

      public int getAttackRange()
      Retrieves the maximum distance this unit can launch an attack from its current tile.
      Returns:
      The attack range.

    • setAttackRange

      public void setAttackRange(int attackRange)
      Sets the unit's targeting range. Clamps the value between 0 and the maximum dimensions of the board.
      Parameters:
      attackRange - The new attack range value.

    • getImpactRadius

      public int getImpactRadius()
      Retrieves the radius of the Area of Effect (AoE) for this unit's standard attack.
      Returns:
      The impact radius size.

    • setImpactRadius

      public void setImpactRadius(int impactRadius)
      Sets the AoE impact radius for the unit's attacks. Ensures the stat cannot drop below 0.
      Parameters:
      impactRadius - The new impact radius size.

    • isDead

      public boolean isDead()
      Evaluates whether the unit's current hit points have dropped to or below zero.
      Returns:
      true if the unit is dead, false otherwise.

    • canMove

      public boolean canMove()
      Checks if the unit is legally allowed to perform a movement action this turn.
      Returns:
      true if the unit has not moved yet and is not an immobile obstacle.

    • setHasMoved

      public void setHasMoved(boolean hasMoved)
      Flags whether the unit has completed its movement action for the current turn.
      Parameters:
      hasMoved - true to lock movement, false to unlock it.

    • canAct

      public boolean canAct()
      Checks if the unit is legally allowed to execute a combat action this turn.
      Returns:
      true if the unit has not acted yet and is not an immobile obstacle.

    • setHasActed

      public void setHasActed(boolean hasActed)
      Flags whether the unit has executed an attack action. Note: Taking an action automatically forfeits any remaining movement for the turn.
      Parameters:
      hasActed - true to lock action state, false to unlock it.

    • resetTurnAction

      public void resetTurnAction()
      Re-enables the unit's ability to move and attack for a new turn.

    • getMoveType

      public MoveStrategy getMoveType()
      Retrieves the movement behavior strategy attached to this unit (e.g., Walking, Flying).
      Returns:
      The specific MoveStrategy implemented by the unit.

    • setMoveType

      public void setMoveType(MoveStrategy moveType)
      Assigns a movement behavior strategy to dictate how this unit traverses the grid.
      Parameters:
      moveType - The specific MoveStrategy to attach.

    • getAttackType

      public AttackStrategy getAttackType()
      Retrieves the targeting behavior strategy attached to this unit (e.g., Melee, Ranged).
      Returns:
      The specific AttackStrategy implemented by the unit.

    • setAttackType

      public void setAttackType(AttackStrategy attackType)
      Assigns an attack behavior strategy to dictate how this unit targets enemies.
      Parameters:
      attackType - The specific AttackStrategy to attach.

    • getPassiveSkills

      public ArrayList<PassiveSkill> getPassiveSkills()
      Retrieves the list of inherent passive skills this unit possesses.
      Returns:
      The list of attached PassiveSkills.

    • triggerPassives

      public void triggerPassives(PassiveType currentPhase)
      Scans the unit's passive abilities and executes any that are bound to the specified trigger phase.
      Parameters:
      currentPhase - The game phase acting as the trigger for the passives.

    • getLingeringEffects

      public ArrayList<LingeringEffect> getLingeringEffects()
      Retrieves the queue of active, turn-based status effects currently afflicting this unit.
      Returns:
      The list of active LingeringEffects.

    • addEffect

      public void addEffect(CombatEffect effect)
      Applies a combat effect to the unit. Instant effects modify the unit immediately, whereas lingering effects are queued to resolve over multiple turns.
      Parameters:
      effect - The incoming CombatEffect to process.

    • processLingeringEffects

      public void processLingeringEffects()
      Iterates through the unit's active status queue. Applies the effect's impact, decrements its duration counter, and cleans it from the queue if the duration expires. Should be called automatically during the Resolution phase.

    • isObstacle

      public boolean isObstacle()
      Checks if this unit is classified as an environmental obstacle rather than a combatant.
      Returns:
      true if the unit is an obstacle, false if it is a standard entity.

    • setObstacle

      public void setObstacle(boolean obstacle)
      Sets whether this unit is classified as an environmental obstacle.
      Parameters:
      obstacle - true to mark as an obstacle, false otherwise.

    • isTown

      public boolean isTown()
      Checks if this unit is specifically classified as a Town structure.
      Returns:
      true if the unit is a town, false otherwise.

    • setTown

      public void setTown(boolean town)
      Sets whether this unit is classified as a Town structure.
      Parameters:
      town - true to mark as a town, false otherwise.

    • isBurning

      public boolean isBurning()
      Checks the lingering effects queue to determine if the unit is currently on fire.
      Specified by:
      isBurning in interface Flammable
      Returns:
      true if an active BurnEffect is present, false otherwise.

    • isFlammable

      public boolean isFlammable()
      Evaluates if this unit is susceptible to catching fire based on its composition and passive resistances.
      Specified by:
      isFlammable in interface Flammable
      Returns:
      true if the unit can be ignited, false if it is immune or an invalid target.

    • ignite

      public void ignite()
      Sets the unit on fire by adding a BurnEffect to its queue, provided it is flammable and not currently protected by airborne status (FlyingMovement).
      Specified by:
      ignite in interface Flammable

    • burn

      public void burn()
      Defined by the Flammable interface. For BaseUnits, the burn logic is instead handled by the processLingeringEffects() method triggering the tick() of a BurnEffect.
      Specified by:
      burn in interface Flammable

    • extinguish

      public void extinguish()
      Extinguishes flames on the unit by purging any BurnEffects from its lingering effects queue.
      Specified by:
      extinguish in interface Flammable

    • takeDamage

      public void takeDamage(int amount)
      Calculates and applies incoming damage to the unit. Automatically subtracts the unit's defense stat from the incoming amount (minimum 0 damage taken) and logs the result.
      Parameters:
      amount - The raw incoming damage amount before defense mitigation.

    • omaeWaMouShindeiru

      public void omaeWaMouShindeiru()
      Instantly kills the unit by dropping its hit points to exactly zero. (NANI?!)

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Generates a string representation of the unit's core stats and positional data. Useful for system debugging and logging.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A formatted string detailing the unit's state.
